$comment: PRD §17 R8 — every GUI capability is reachable from tt. This is a GOLD fixture asserted complete by packages/gui/test/parity.test.ts: each GUI IPC channel (a capability) maps to a tt command that actually exists in the CLI program. Parity is a coverage claim, not a behaviour (acceptance.html §05 'On parity'). NOTE (§19 R03): the in-app update check is a GUI/OS-only capability with NO tt equivalent (a CLI install is updated by the package manager/installer), exactly like the tray and the global hotkey, so — by design — it is NOT in ipc.ts CHANNELS and has NO row here. It lives on a separate update:getVersion / update:check IPC surface (main.ts registerUpdateIpc, bridged as window.stint.update), kept off the parity-asserted channel set; it is covered by MANUAL (runbook 'CHECK SOFTWARE UPDATE — CHECK FOR UPDATES (§19 R03)') + GOLD (packages/gui/test/update.test.ts).

capabilities.description: see the running timer, entries, and current settings — the single snapshot the renderer paints (status + list + config ls, folded into one read)
§09 R7 — free-text search over the day-grouped history (description / client / project / tag, case-insensitive); narrows the visible list, parity with tt list/report --search

§12 R9 — the Entries view's control bar: group the entry list by day/client/project/tag, narrow by a range preset (or custom range) + client/project/tag/billable, and free-text search — all over core's buildEntryList; read-only, parity with tt list --range/--client/--project/--tag/--search --by
global-hotkey / tray toggle of the timer
start a new entry / Switch (atomic stop+start)
stop the open entry
resume the last entry
backfill a completed entry from explicit from/to
amend any field of an entry
split an entry at an instant
merge a contiguous selection
delete an entry
exclude slept time (reversible)

grouped totals, filters, rounding, on-screen summary with flags; date-range presets (today/week/last-week/month/last-month) + custom from/to — the preset travels inside the report payload and is resolved through core's resolveRange, no new channel
§09 R08 — save a named report definition (range-spec / group-by / filters / rounding) from the Reports view's builder; delegates to core store.saveReport, parity with tt report save
§09 R08 — list the saved report definitions (the Reports view's saved-definitions rail); parity with tt report ls
§09 R08 — inspect one saved report definition; parity with tt report show
§09 R08 — rename a saved report; parity with tt report rename
§09 R08 — amend a saved report's range/grouping/filters/rounding from the builder; parity with tt report edit
§09 R08 — delete a saved report; parity with tt report rm

§09 R09 — run a saved report against current data and render grouped totals (the same core Report payload the ad-hoc report channel builds, resolved through core's resolveSavedRange); parity with tt report run

§09 R6 — write the raw entries for a range to a CSV / JSON file via the OS save dialog; renders the bytes through core's toCsv/toJsonEntries (byte-identical to tt export) since the renderer cannot touch fs

§05 R09 — pin a favorite (a named timer template) from the running entry, a closed entry, or explicit attributes, from the Timer view's favorites rail; delegates to core store.pinFavorite, parity with tt fav add
§05 R09 — list the pinned favorites (the Timer view's favorites rail); parity with tt fav ls
§05 R09 — rename a favorite; parity with tt fav rename
§05 R09 — unpin (delete) a favorite; parity with tt fav rm

§05 R10 — resume from a favorite: one action starts a FRESH timer from the favorite's template (atomic stop-then-start, overlap warned not blocked, the favorite unchanged), from the Timer view's favorites rail; delegates to core store.startFromFavorite, parity with tt fav start / tt start --fav
create a client
create a project
list clients
rename a client
archive a client (reversible hide)
rename a project
archive a project (reversible hide)
list a client's projects
§12 R10 — list the active tags (the Clients view's tag strip)
§12 R10 — create a tag (create-or-return; the explicit manage-it-first path)
§12 R10 — rename a tag
§12 R10 — archive a tag (reversible hide from pickers, keeps history)

§12 R11 / §14 — change any §14 setting from the GUI Settings view (rounding, rounding increment, week start, first check-in, check-in interval, global hotkey, accent usage, date format, backup retention); the report view's rounding controls ride the same channel — parity with tt config set

§20 R04 / §17 R12 — list the automatic timestamped backups beside the database (the Settings → Backups restore list + 'Last backup' status); delegates to core store.listBackups, parity with tt backup ls
§20 R05 / §17 R12 — restore the database from a chosen backup (the Settings → Restore… action): core quarantines the current file and re-points the store at the backup; parity with tt backup restore
